meta.name: Miso Soup
meta.subtitle: Classic dashi + miso broth with tofu and wakame

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Uses miso added off-heat to preserve aroma and avoid curdling/bitterness.

yield.notes: Serves 4 small bowls as a side soup.

signals.notes: Best fresh; refrigerate up to 2 days. Avoid boiling after miso is added.

tasteProfile.balanceNotes: Adjust miso amount to taste; dashi provides the base umami.

notes: For vegetarian/vegan: use kombu + dried shiitake dashi (or instant vegan dashi).
For gluten-free: verify miso and dashi are gluten-free (some contain barley/wheat).

ingredients.label: Dashi stock (prepared)
ingredients.notes: Use this instead of water if you already have dashi. If using water, make quick dashi below.

ingredients.label: Instant dashi powder/granules
ingredients.notes: Follow package strength; optional if using prepared dashi.

ingredients.label: Kombu (dried kelp)
ingredients.notes: For a simple kombu-forward dashi; remove before simmering hard.

ingredients.label: Katsuobushi (bonito flakes)
ingredients.notes: Optional if making quick dashi (kombu + bonito). Strain after steeping.

ingredients.label: Miso paste (white/shiro or awase blend)
ingredients.notes: About 3 Tbsp. Adjust to taste; different misos vary in saltiness.

ingredients.label: Daikon, thin matchsticks or half-moons
ingredients.notes: Optional add-in; simmer until just tender before adding miso.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional: a small splash to deepen savoriness if your dashi is mild.

steps.title: Build the broth (dashi)
instructions: If using prepared dashi: pour into a saucepan and warm gently until steaming (do not boil hard).
If using water + instant dashi: bring water to a bare simmer and dissolve dashi granules; keep at a gentle simmer.
If making quick kombu + bonito dashi: warm water with kombu until steaming (before boiling). Remove kombu, then add bonito flakes, simmer 30 seconds, turn off heat, steep 2–3 minutes, then strain.
checks: Broth is hot and aromatic, not aggressively boiling

steps.title: Cook optional vegetables (if using)
instructions: If adding shiitake or daikon, simmer them in the broth until just tender.
Keep the heat gentle to preserve clarity and avoid reducing too much.

steps.title: Add tofu and wakame
instructions: Add cubed tofu and gently warm through (avoid vigorous boiling so tofu stays intact).
Add dried wakame and let it rehydrate for 30–60 seconds.

steps.title: Dissolve miso off-heat (do not boil)
instructions: Turn heat to low or off so the broth is hot but not boiling.
In a small bowl (or ladle), whisk a scoop of hot broth into the miso paste to dissolve it smoothly.
Stir the dissolved miso back into the pot. Taste and adjust: add a bit more miso or a small splash of soy sauce if needed.
Keep the soup below a simmer after miso is added.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Ladle into bowls and top with sliced green onion.
Serve immediately as a side with rice, noodles, or a main dish.

pairings.notes: Miso soup is a classic supporting bowl for many Japanese meals.

refrigerator.notes: Reheat gently; do not boil. Add fresh scallions at serving.

freezer.notes: Texture of tofu and wakame changes; miso aroma dulls.

methods: Stovetop on low until steaming (do not boil)
reheat.notes: If the soup reaches a boil, flavor can become harsh and aroma diminishes.

planner.weeknightNotes: Use instant dashi and pre-cut tofu to make this a 10–15 minute soup.

notes: Miso varies widely: start with less, then add more to taste.
Never boil miso soup after adding miso—keep it steaming-hot only.
For a richer bowl: add clams, thin sliced cabbage, or a spoon of sesame paste (non-traditional but tasty).
Use kombu + dried shiitake to build the broth (soak dried shiitake in warm water 10–20 minutes, then heat gently).
Use red miso for deeper flavor; reduce quantity slightly at first (it can be saltier).
Add daikon and mushrooms for a more robust bowl.

summary: Classic Japanese miso soup with hot dashi broth, tofu, wakame, and scallions. Miso is dissolved off-heat to preserve aroma and avoid harshness.

foodSafety: Reheat to steaming-hot; avoid prolonged boiling.
allergenNotes: May contain fish (dashi) and gluten depending on ingredients.
